method_atom: A numerical many-body simulation of a large ensemble of particles interacting through a long-range field governed by a Poisson-type equation, in which the standard potential is augmented by an additional density-dependent scalar force that enhances aggregation, especially on scales that have undergone mild nonlinear processing. The approach is paired with an empirically modified fitting formula that maps the linear two-point power spectrum of the fluctuation field to its nonlinear counterpart, correcting a prior formula that overestimates the enhancement.
object_atom: The gravitational clustering of cosmic matter into large-scale structure under theories with infinite-volume extra dimensions, specifically the Dvali-Gabadadze-Porrati (DGP) model and its higher-dimensional generalizations where 4D gravity is mediated by massive gravitons whose longitudinal mode adds an extra force, constrained against galaxy redshift survey power spectra.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the many-body simulation of particles under a density-dependent modification of a Poisson-governed long-range force plus an empirical nonlinear power-spectrum fitting formula; domain-specific is the modified-gravity cosmological structure formation (DGP/massive-graviton) tested on galaxy surveys.
